summary.name: Hurst-Persistence Moving-Average Equity Trend Strategy
summary.author: Ahmet Kasti
summary.description: The strategy is an equity trend-following model that seeks to capture persistent price trends by combining a persistence metric (Hurst exponent) with a directional trend filter (moving average). summary.description_full:
The strategy is an equity trend-following model that seeks to capture persistent price trends by combining a persistence metric (Hurst exponent) with a directional trend filter (moving average). It targets market conditions where returns exhibit serial dependence and sustained momentum, favoring liquid names where trends are more likely to be exploitable after costs. The investable universe is refreshed monthly from US equities using fundamental data, filtering out low-priced stocks and selecting the top 200 by dollar trading volume to maintain liquidity and capacity.

Signals are evaluated on daily bars but portfolio decisions occur monthly (at the start of each month). A stock is eligible if its price is above its medium-term exponential moving average (trend up) and its Hurst exponent over a medium lookback exceeds a fixed threshold indicating persistence. From eligible names, the portfolio selects up to five stocks with the highest persistence scores and allocates equal weights across them, targeting full notional exposure. If no names qualify, the strategy holds existing positions (no forced de-risk), implying risk is primarily controlled via concentrated position count, liquidity constraints, and systematic monthly rebalancing. The intended alpha source is behavioral and microstructural trend persistence as proxied by the Hurst exponent, conditioned on positive trend direction.
